app.name: vlt
app.one_liner: A modular, TypeScript-based package manager with secure caching, dependency graph resolution, and CLI tooling.

screens.name: CLI Interface
screens.purpose: Primary entry point for users to execute package management commands such as install, uninstall, and view.

rust_commands.name: hydrate_cli_options
rust_commands.purpose: Populate CLI command options from configuration or defaults during execution.

rust_commands.name: uninstall_package
rust_commands.purpose: Remove a package from the project, handling cases where only a lockfile exists.

mvp_plan: week 1: Ensure CLI hydration works with minimal project state
week 1: Implement core uninstall logic resilient to missing vlt.json
week 1: Validate lockfile-only scenarios in integration tests
acceptance_tests: CLI options are correctly hydrated from available configuration sources during command execution.
Default secure configuration entries are applied automatically if vlt.json is missing.
The `vlt uninstall` command succeeds when only vlt-lock.json is present and no vlt.json exists.
